title: Introduction and Scope
objective: Define the review scope covering pedestrian behavior modeling at signalized intersections and unsignalized crosswalks, establishing the comparative framework for examining methodological approaches and claim conflicts

title: Microscopic Simulation Models for Pedestrian Behavior
objective: Synthesize microscopic simulation approaches including agent-based force models and pedestrian type classification systems, examining their applicability to signal compliance and crossing behavior

title: Statistical and Econometric Models for Crossing Behavior
objective: Compare statistical mixture models, random parameter logit models, and discrete choice models for analyzing pedestrian waiting times, red light violation propensity, and speed change events

title: Probabilistic and Machine Learning Approaches
objective: Evaluate Dynamic Bayesian Network models with particle filtering for pedestrian crossing decision recognition and motion classification, comparing with regression-based prediction methods

title: Experimental and Field Study Methods
objective: Review VR-based experiments for autonomous vehicle-pedestrian interaction, field experiments on mobile phone distraction effects, and video observation methodologies for behavior analysis

title: Geographic Context and Cross-Cultural Applicability
objective: Examine the geographic distribution of study sites (China, Japan, Hong Kong, India), assess generalizability claims, and address the verified gap regarding Chinese-context representation

title: Claim Conflicts and Methodological Discrepancies
objective: Analyze the 41 documented contradictions across papers including metric inconsistencies (MAPE vs modified_rho_squared), parameter value differences (r2=0.69 vs 0.35), and conflicting findings on waiting time distributions

title: Synthesis and Recommendations
objective: Provide integrated findings across methodological families, identify gaps in current research, and propose standardized comparison criteria for future pedestrian behavior studies
